_comment: Registry of known sources and the confidence level scripts/refresh_feed.py should assign them by default. This is the actual governance artifact behind spec §14's 'never present aggregated/scraped info as official unless it comes directly from an official source' - a collector should look a source up here rather than deciding confidence ad hoc per listing.

sources.name: ReliefWeb
sources.notes: UN OCHA-run job board aggregating official postings from UN agencies and major NGOs - treated as official because listings link back to the hiring organisation's own posting, not a third-party repost.

sources.name: YALI Network
sources.notes: U.S. Department of State's own fellowship platform.

sources.name: Employer career page
sources.notes: Any listing fetched directly from an organisation's own /careers or /jobs page - confidence comes from the fetch target, not a fixed domain list.

sources.name: BrighterMonday
sources.notes: Reputable Kenyan job board, but a third-party repost - never the employer's own page.

sources.name: Newsletter or WhatsApp forward
sources.notes: No independent way to verify the original poster - mark community, never aggregated or official, regardless of how the content reads.
